summary: Iteration 3 testing of Dine-In vs Takeaway bill flow + PWA regression. Backend: 10/10 new tests passed (100%), including takeaway creation, dine-in creation, 400 on missing table_id, list filter by order_type, full pipelines for both types (add items → send KOT → chef ready/served → cashier payment), tables endpoint never attaches takeaway bills to tables, analytics summary+CSV regression, and cashier totals regression. Frontend: PWA manifest + service-worker served (200 OK), public/index.html references manifest and src/index.js registers SW in production, owner dashboard loads without blank screen, /orders/takeaway route works with takeaway filter showing TAKEAWAY-badged bills, and navigation links (Dashboard, Tables, Take-away, Staff, Menu, Inventory, Orders, Settings) all render correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1000 lines; consider splitting into routers (auth, bills, menu, analytics) for maintainability but not blocking.
create_bill returns an EXISTING open bill for the same table (dine-in) instead of 409/400; this is current design and frontend relies on it, keep as-is but document.
takeaway bills are never occupied on any table — verified via GET /api/tables attachment logic works correctly.

NewOrderDialog is wired only on captain/cashier screens; owner's /owner/tables is the admin (edit/delete) view — NewOrderDialog not reachable from owner UI directly. Not a bug per spec but worth confirming with PO if owners should also be able to create orders directly from Owner UI.
seed_data_creation: Tests create a few TEST_ prefixed bills & tables and cancel/delete them where possible. Some closed takeaway test bills remain in DB (by design, they are closed/paid and represent realistic data).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend bill schema now includes `order_type` ('dine_in'|'takeaway'), takeaway bills have table_id=null and table_name='TAKEAWAY'. list_bills accepts `order_type` query param. PWA manifest.json & service-worker.js served from /, SW registers only in production build. Console shows benign 401s on /api/auth/me before login. Captain login is NOT geofence-blocked by default (geofence_enabled=false in seeded settings). test_takeaway_dinein.py is the regression harness for this iteration and should be run alongside test_iteration3_api.py.
